chief_complaint: A postpartum woman presents with symptoms of acute coronary syndrome three weeks after a Caesarean section.
history: The patient is a postpartum woman who had a Caesarean section three weeks prior to presentation. She now presents with symptoms suggestive of acute coronary syndrome.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are provided.
labs: No specific laboratory or imaging results are provided, but the patient is diagnosed with spontaneous coronary artery dissection (SCAD).
